statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity accesscontrol 3.0.11 finding. AccessControl 3.0.11 — Zope's role/permission engine. The 1.3 image pins AccessControl 3.0.11 (vs 4.2 in the 2.0 image); the advisory's exploit path needs principal review of role-evaluation under Plone 4.3.20.

High-severity cairosvg 1.0.20 SSRF/XXE-class finding. CairoSVG is pulled in by senaite.core 1.3.5's WeasyPrint 0.42.3 hard pin, used for PDF rendering of LIMS reports/labels. Server-side SVG rendering against authenticated-only LIMS report data is the relevant deployment path; pending principal review of whether report templates can embed externally-fetched URI references and the CairoSVG version's url_fetcher configuration. Same review context as the 2.0.0 image's cairosvg statements — both versions are the last 2.7-compatible release.

statements.status_notes: certifi 2021.10.8 ships as the requests/urllib3 trust store. The advisory covers a CA root included in the bundle. SENAITE LIMS deployments only make outbound TLS connections to operator-configured endpoints (admin sets the URL when configuring senaite.jsonapi/REST integrations), and the runtime never receives connections from arbitrary remote TLS servers — there is no path through which an attacker controls the server presenting a compromised-CA-issued certificate to senaite-lims-1.3. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot upgrade certifi past 2021.10.8 (requests 2.27.1's last 2.7-compatible pin).

statements.impact_statement: High-severity feedparser 5.0.1 finding. feedparser 5.0.1 is a Python 2 RSS/Atom parser. Plone 4.3 uses it for syndication content types. Pending principal review of whether SENAITE 1.3 enables any feed-rendering content type (likely not — SENAITE's UI is laboratory data, not feeds).

High-severity future 0.13.1 finding. `future` 0.13.1 is a Python 2/3 compatibility shim, transitively pulled in. The 1.3 image's pin is OLDER than the 2.0 image's 0.18.2. Pending principal review to enumerate which future-supplied modules SENAITE actually imports and whether any execute against untrusted input.

Medium-severity idna 2.10 finding. idna is consumed transitively by requests/urllib3 for hostname encoding. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past idna 2.10 (last 2.7-compatible release). Pending principal review of whether SENAITE accepts hostnames from untrusted callers.
Negligible-severity libblkid1 2.41-5 finding. libblkid is part of util-linux's block-device probing. The senaite-lims-1.3 runtime never probes block devices — no exec path invokes blkid.

statements.status_notes: Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. glob() recursion limit; documented POSIX behaviour and Debian flags this as unimportant. SENAITE never invokes glob() against attacker-supplied patterns — Plone catalog queries use Zope's own ZCatalog query language, not C-library globbing. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. glibc regex stack-consumption against attacker-controlled patterns; upstream disputes. Python 2.7's `re` module uses its own engine, not glibc regex; SENAITE/Plone never call libc regex via ctypes. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. stack-guard-bypass disclosure that upstream classifies as a non-security bug. The senaite-lims-1.3 runtime has no setuid binaries and no exposed listener at the libc layer; the prerequisite primitive does not exist on this image. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. Requires the local user to run `ldd` on a malicious ELF; glibc upstream classifies this as non-security. The senaite-lims-1.3 runtime is distroless (no shell, no `ldd`); an interactive caller cannot execute the vulnerable program. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. ASLR-bypass disclosure; upstream states ASLR weakening is not itself a vulnerability. Exploitation requires a separate primitive (setuid binary or exposed listener) that this image does not provide. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. ASLR bypass for pthread_create thread heap; upstream disputes. No exploit primitive exists on the senaite-lims-1.3 image — the runtime user is unprivileged uid 65532 and there is no setuid surface. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

Inherited from python-2.7.vex.json. Uncontrolled-recursion in glibc regex compilation; upstream disputes. Python 2.7 ships its own `re` engine; SENAITE/Plone do not call glibc regcomp/regexec via ctypes. The senaite-lims-1.3 image runs the Plone bin/instance HTTP server on top of the same python-2.7 base, with no additional code path that reaches the vulnerable glibc primitive. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the lower-layer statement.

High-severity libexpat1 2.7.1-2 finding. libexpat is consumed by Python's pyexpat for XML parsing. SENAITE/Plone use lxml (libxml2) for primary XML parsing; pyexpat sees occasional internal use through stdlib (e.g. plistlib, XML-RPC). Pending principal review of whether any reachable XML-parsing path is fed adversary-controlled input — defaulting to under_investigation for the 1.3 image since the audit has not yet been completed.

High-severity mechanize 0.2.5 finding. mechanize 0.2.5 is a Python 2 stateful web-browser library used by Plone's older HTTP-test infrastructure (CMFPlone test suite). Pending principal review of whether mechanize is invoked at runtime — typically test-only, but Plone 4.3.20's PageTemplate engine sometimes imports it transitively. If runtime-only-imported-not-invoked, this becomes a future not_affected statement.

Critical-severity Pillow 3.3.3 image-parsing finding. Pillow is the server-side image processor for sample uploads (microscopy, gels) by authenticated lab technicians. Pillow 3.3.3 is OLDER than the 2.0.0 image's 6.2.2 — more code paths are reachable, not fewer. Heritage Python 2.7 cannot move past Pillow 6.2.2 (the last 2.7-compatible release); senaite.core 1.3.5 in fact pins to 3.3.3 specifically. The upload pipeline accepts files from authenticated users, so the attack surface is real. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING until the upload pipeline's MIME whitelist, size cap, and processing-isolation strategy are documented for the 1.3 deployment.

statements.status_notes: tarfile path-traversal (Trail of Bits, 2007). CPython 2.7.18 is the final 2.7 release; wellmaintained's locally-built python-2.7 image carries the upstream fix as part of the 2.7.18 source tree. SENAITE further reduces the surface by extracting tarballs only at build time (zc.buildout download cache) — the senaite-lims-1.3 runtime has no tarfile.extract() call path.

Disputed: Tk webbrowser-launching argument-injection. Requires `webbrowser.open()` with attacker-controlled URL. SENAITE 1.3.5 is a server-side Plone application; no webbrowser.open() call exists in the runtime. The senaite-lims-1.3 image has no _tkinter and no display.

Disputed: Wave.open() crash on crafted .wav. SENAITE/Plone never invoke wave.open(); the senaite-lims-1.3 runtime processes only HTTP requests via bin/instance and delegates image processing to Pillow (covered separately) — wave is not on any code path.

urllib.parse_qsl semicolon-as-separator finding. Plone 4.3.20's ZPublisher uses Zope's own request parser, not urllib.parse_qsl, for query-string handling. The vulnerable parser receives only operator-curated input where SENAITE invokes it (URL-fragment parsing in senaite.jsonapi result-link generation) — no anonymous-user-controlled string reaches it.

ctypes.PyCArg_repr buffer overflow. Exploitation requires Python code calling repr() on a c_double constructed from an attacker-controlled float. SENAITE/Plone code paths construct c_double only via cairocffi/cffi for cairo bindings (PDF rendering); those never call repr() on the wrapper, and the inputs are operator-defined report-template constants.

urllib.request AbstractBasicAuthHandler ReDoS on crafted Authentication header. SENAITE makes outbound HTTP only via the requests stack (covered separately); urllib.request is not the active client. Even when invoked transitively, the Authentication header is server-supplied from operator-configured outbound endpoints — not adversary-controlled.

urllib.parse newline-injection finding. Plone 4.3.20 uses Zope's ZPublisher request parser, not urllib.parse, for incoming URLs. Outbound URL construction in SENAITE uses operator-configured endpoint strings — adversary cannot inject control characters into those.
IDNA decoder DoS on crafted domain names. SENAITE outbound HTTP uses the requests/urllib3 stack against operator-configured endpoints; the domain name is never adversary-controlled.

heapq.heappushpop use-after-free. SENAITE/Plone code paths do not invoke heapq with adversary-controlled list mutations; the closest use is within ZODB's internal indexing, where the input is server-controlled.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity CPython 2.7.18 finding. Pending principal review of which stdlib module the advisory covers and whether SENAITE/Plone invoke that module against adversary-controlled input. Candidate for inheritance to common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json once analysis completes.

statements.status_notes: plistlib XML external entity expansion. SENAITE/Plone never call plistlib at runtime — the format is Apple property lists, which are not part of the Plone request, persistence, or report-rendering pipeline.

hmac compare_digest constant-time bypass. The function is used by Plone's CSRF and password-hash comparisons against operator-defined keys; the timing side-channel requires per-comparison adversary input that is not exposed by any SENAITE 1.3 endpoint.

urllib.parse.urlparse blocklist-bypass via leading whitespace. Plone 4.3.20 uses Zope's request parser, not urllib.parse, for incoming URL handling; SENAITE's outbound URL construction uses operator-configured strings.

email.utils.parseaddr regex parser-confusion. SENAITE generates email From/To headers from authenticated lab-staff records; the parseaddr inputs are LIMS-database-curated, not adversary-supplied at runtime.
email.parser RFC-5322 stack overflow. SENAITE invokes email.parser only when sending lab-result emails to operator-configured SMTP recipients; the input is server-generated, not adversary-supplied.

ssl.SSLSocket pre-handshake-data smuggling. The senaite-lims-1.3 runtime accepts inbound HTTP via bin/instance's classic ZServer (TLS terminated at the reverse proxy in the standard SENAITE deployment), not directly via Python's ssl module. The pre-handshake-data path is unreachable.

tempfile.TemporaryDirectory symlink-attack on cleanup. The senaite-lims-1.3 runtime filesystem is read-only at OCI mount time except for /opt/senaite-1.3/var (ZODB + logs), which the unprivileged senaite user owns; no other process writes to /tmp where this attack would land.
ssl.SSLContext memory-corruption via concurrent use. SENAITE makes outbound TLS through requests/urllib3 with per-request connection pooling; no shared SSLContext is mutated across threads.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity CPython 2.7.18 finding. Pending principal review of which stdlib module the advisory covers and whether SENAITE/Plone invoke that module against adversary-controlled input. Candidate for inheritance to common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json once analysis completes.

statements.status_notes: urllib email-address parser injection. Same reasoning as CVE-2023-27043: SENAITE's email-utils inputs are LIMS-database-curated, not adversary-supplied.
tarfile attribute-modification via crafted member. SENAITE/Plone do not invoke tarfile at runtime; archive extraction occurs only at build time within zc.buildout's download cache.

ipaddress.is_private misclassification of certain IPv4 ranges. SENAITE makes outbound HTTP to operator-configured endpoints; the is_private check is consulted (if at all) on operator-defined hostnames, not adversary input.

statements.status_notes: tarfile ReDoS in PAX header parsing. SENAITE/Plone never invoke tarfile at runtime; the runtime image filesystem is read-only and there is no archive-handling endpoint.

email module header-injection. SENAITE's email payloads (lab-result notifications) are constructed from operator-configured templates with LIMS-database values; no anonymous user input reaches header construction.

http.cookies CookieJar quadratic complexity on crafted cookie. SENAITE outbound HTTP via requests/urllib3 is to operator-configured endpoints; their cookies are server-supplied, not adversary-controlled. Inbound cookies in Plone 4.3 go through Zope's ZPublisher cookie parser, not http.cookies.
zipfile.ZipFile DoS on crafted ZIP. SENAITE/Plone do not invoke zipfile at runtime — buildout-driven zip extraction occurs only at build time.

venv activation-script command-injection. The senaite-lims-1.3 image does not use venv at runtime — the Python environment is the python-2.7 base image's site-packages, materialised at build time. No `python -m venv` invocation exists in the runtime.
urllib.parse hostname canonicalisation finding. Plone uses Zope's ZPublisher request parser, not urllib.parse; SENAITE outbound URL targets are operator-configured.

statements.status_notes: tarfile filter bypass (recent CPython advisory). SENAITE/Plone do not invoke tarfile at runtime — archive extraction occurs only at build time inside zc.buildout's download cache, before the senaite-lims-1.3 runtime image is assembled. The runtime entrypoint is bin/instance; tarfile is never imported.

High-severity RestrictedPython 3.6.0 sandbox-escape finding. RestrictedPython is Plone's TAL/Through-The-Web sandbox; SENAITE 1.3 allows Manager-role users to author TTW Python scripts via Plone's Script (Python) content type. RestrictedPython 3.6.0 is OLDER than the 2.0.0 image's 5.1 — more sandbox-escape paths are reachable, not fewer. The exploit path requires Manager role (a trusted role per Plone's ACL), but Manager-authored scripts that other roles trigger remain security-relevant. PRINCIPAL-REVIEW-BLOCKING.

statements.status_notes: setuptools 44.1.1 is inherited from the python-2.7 base image (last 2.7-compatible release) and used at senaite-lims-1.3 build time only (zc.buildout pre-installs transitive deps). The runtime entrypoint is /opt/senaite-1.3/bin/instance (Plone Zope2 server); the image filesystem is read-only and there is no scheduler or shell that exec's setuptools. The vulnerable code path never fires on the deployed container. See common/images/python-2.7/python-2.7.vex.json for the same finding at the lower layer.

statements.impact_statement: Medium-severity simplejson 2.5.2 finding. simplejson 2.5.2 is a Python 2 JSON parser used by Zope and senaite.jsonapi. Pending principal review of advisory coverage at this older pin.

High-severity WeasyPrint 0.42.3 finding. WeasyPrint generates PDF reports/labels for SENAITE; the rendering path includes URL fetching for CSS/images. WeasyPrint 0.42.3 is older than the 2.0.0 image's WeasyPrint pin (senaite.core 1.3.5 hard-pins 0.42.3, the last release supporting Python 2). Pending principal review of url_fetcher configuration and which SENAITE report templates can embed externally-fetched URI references. Tightly coupled to the cairosvg findings.

statements.status_notes: werkzeug 1.0.1 is a Flask-stack WSGI utility; SENAITE 1.3.5 runs on Zope 2.13.30's classic ZServer (bin/instance), not Flask or any WSGI bridge. werkzeug is in the SBOM as a transitive dep of Plone-test or zope.interface support code, but its HTTP request/response objects are never instantiated at runtime — Plone 4.3.20 uses Zope's own ZPublisher exclusively. The 1.3 image is even narrower than the 2.0 image on this point: there is no waitress or WSGI optionality in the buildout.
